warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose: The Fukui reactivity calculation for ferrocene was carried out using the semi-empirical quantum chemistry method GFN2-xTB in the gas phase, with charge 0 and multiplicity 1. Atomic indices were determined to identify reactivity by analyzing the Fukui functions (f⁺ for electrophilic attack areas, f⁻ for nucleophilic attack areas, and f⁰ for areas with dual attack propensity). The most nucleophilic site in ferrocene is the iron atom (Fe) at index 10 with a Fukui f⁻ value of 0.25938191. This is the headline result we report. The CLI invocation tracked was `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method xtb --charge 0 --mult 1 --solvent none /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp0r4pouig/live.json`. Warnings included: 'sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)' and 'sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge).' The integrity of the result was verified with status 'trustworthy.'
